description: Sobh-e Emroz (صبح امروز, 'This Morning') was founded by [Saeed Hajjarian](/people/saeed-hajjarian), Khatami's chief political strategist and a former deputy Minister of Intelligence. It was the investigative flagship of the reformist press, publishing [Akbar Ganji](/people/akbar-ganji) and [Emadeddin Baghi](/people/emadeddin-baghi)'s landmark series on the [chain murders of 1998](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) using code names ('Excellency Red Garmented' for Rafsanjani, 'Master Key' for [Ali Fallahian](/people/ali-fallahian)). This reporting forced the government to admit Ministry of Intelligence 'rogue elements' were responsible. On 12 March 2000, Hajjarian was shot in the face outside Tehran City Council by a Basij gunman, leaving him permanently disabled. Sobh-e Emroz was among the 16 papers closed in the 24 April 2000 crackdown.
